what_it_does: Implements a pure, deterministic resolver that computes concrete values from an invariant dict of slots and a coordinate dict. Each slot is either a continuous relationship AST evaluated by runtime.rules.evaluate, a discrete registry selection keyed by a coordinate path, or a literal; malformed slots raise ResolverError.
observations: Uses runtime.rules.evaluate for AST evaluation and rules._get_path for dot-path reads, avoiding reimplementation.
resolve_slot distinguishes three slot kinds by inspecting dict keys: 'op' for AST, 'select' for registry, otherwise it raises.
Select slots require a non-empty 'cases' dict and fail loudly if the coordinate value is missing and no default is provided.
The module includes an operational self-test under __name__ == '__main__' that exercises continuous derivation, discrete selection, layout-fit constraints, and fail-loud errors.
The docstring frames the resolver as the fourth primitive (rules-computation / VERB) and contrasts it with hand-enumerated breakpoints.
standouts: The 'select' construct treats discrete axes (orientation, medium, posture, etc.) as registry lookups rather than continuous functions, with explicit fail-loud behavior.
The self-test asserts that header_h + body_h + chrome equals device height for both portrait and landscape coordinates, encoding a layout-fit invariant.
ResolverError is a ValueError subclass used for all malformed-slot and missing-case failures.
conventions: Pure function style: resolve(invariant, coordinate) -> dict with no IO, time, or randomness.
Fail-loud: malformed slots and unmatched discrete cases raise rather than silently default.
Reuses runtime.rules rather than duplicating expression evaluation logic.
Docstring-heavy; includes an operational self-test under __name__ == '__main__'.
Type hints use Any and dict from typing.
concerns: resolve_slot calls rules._get_path, a private helper, coupling the resolver to rules.py internals.
The select branch accepts any value type as a case key, including unhashable values, which would raise at runtime; no explicit validation.
If a select slot's 'cases' dict is empty or missing, the error is raised only after an isinstance check, with the message embedded in the branch.
The __main__ self-test hard-codes expected numeric results and would need updating if the example invariant changes.
notes: This is the core value-computation primitive: one invariant plus a coordinate yields a concrete surface, with continuous derivation and discrete registry selection handled by a single slot resolver.
questions: What is the full set of operators and value types supported by runtime.rules.evaluate?
How are coordinate paths with missing intermediate keys handled by rules._get_path?
Are there intended consumers that call resolve directly, or is it wrapped by another runtime layer?
Should select case keys be restricted to hashable scalar types?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file resolves invariant slots against coordinates using rules.evaluate and registry selection, exactly as the docstring claims.
intention_signals: To replace hand-written responsive breakpoints and variant files with a single invariant definition that derives every concrete allocation from coordinate axes, using a shared rules engine for continuous dimensions and explicit registry selection for categorical ones.
novelty: The explicit two-axis model: continuous axes resolved by relationship-AST derivation and discrete/categorical axes resolved by registry selection, unified in one slot resolver.
contribution: A deterministic, pure resolver primitive that turns invariant slot definitions into concrete values given a coordinate, separating relationship definitions from coordinate-specific outputs and providing fail-loud error behavior for malformed or unmatched slots.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/resolver.py defines a pure resolver that computes concrete values from an invariant dict of relationship-AST or registry-select slots against a coordinate dict, delegating expression evaluation to runtime.rules and raising ResolverError on malformed or unmatched slots.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, reusable computation primitive that derives all coordinate-specific design allocations from one invariant definition rather than maintaining hand-written breakpoint variants.
proposed_edge_kinds: evaluates_with (slot AST evaluated by rules engine)
reads_path_from (select reads coordinate path via rules._get_path)
raises (ResolverError raised for malformed slot or missing case)

symbols.does: Exception class for malformed invariant slots and unresolved discrete selections.
symbols.description: Subclass of ValueError used by resolve_slot and resolve to fail loudly when a slot is malformed, a select has no matching case, or other invariant/coordinate errors occur.

symbols.code_id: code://company/runtime/resolver.py::resolve_slot | function | resolve_slot(slot: Any, coordinate: dict) -> Any
symbols.does: Resolves one invariant slot against a coordinate.
symbols.description: Inspects the slot: relationship-AST dicts are passed to rules.evaluate, select dicts look up a coordinate path in a cases registry (with optional default), literals are returned as-is, and any other dict raises ResolverError.

symbols.code_id: code://company/runtime/resolver.py::resolve | function | resolve(invariant: dict, coordinate: dict) -> dict
symbols.does: Resolves every slot in an invariant dict against a coordinate.
symbols.description: Validates that invariant and coordinate are dicts, then maps each slot name through resolve_slot, returning a dict of concrete values; any malformed slot causes a loud failure rather than a partial result.
